text: XML Services shall be segmented.
XML Services shall provide infrastructure services for mission and support applications using XML technology.
XML Services shall provide data management functions for access to distributed database management systems.
Data management services shall have the capability of being tuned by on-site personnel in the fixed mode of operation.
XML Services shall redefine or reset names of connect descriptors to database server instances in a dynamic environment.
The XML Services shall reset session time-out values in a degraded communications environment.
Session time-out values shall be user definable.
XML Services shall support managing various types of data and database architectures in Maintenance Mode.
The data management services shall provide production-equivalent processing in Training Mode.
XML Services shall receive XML registry forms.
XML Services shall submit metadata and related information resource artifacts.
XML Services shall produce and display submittal forms as part of the Information Resource Submittal Package from the web.
XML Services shall download Information Resource Submittal Package from the web.
XML Services shall submit prescribed metadata related to information resource type and status code.
XML Services shall submit metadata by an on-line interactive process.
XML Services shall submit metadata by an off-line and interactive batch process.
XML Services shall parse submitted XML Registry specification forms.
XML Services shall populate the XML Registry database.
XML Services shall modify specified terms and definitions of metadata.
XML Services shall associate Information Resources specified in Appendix A.
XML Services shall assemble registered information resources to form new components.
XML Services shall notify changes in Information Resources to authorized producers.
XML Services shall provide a capability to post planned changes to a registry.
XML Services shall approve and reject submissions.
XML Services shall forward requests to different registries.
XML Services shall provide the capability to view a Tag origin relationship.
XML Services shall provide the capability to view a complex Container Tag parent/child relationship.
XML Services shall provide the capability to view a tag data type.
XML Services shall provide the capability to view an Information resource versioning relationship.
XML Services shall view XML Tags via a tree hierarchy structure or tabular format.
XML Services shall view XML Tags via multiple search options.
XML Services shall provide capabilities to download the XML Tags.
XML Services shall process ANSI standard SQL as specified in FIPS PUB 127-2.
XML Services shall establish rules for semantic understanding of terms in an XML document.
XML Services shall provide a capability to subscribe for notification of changes to Communities of Interest.
XML Services shall create and manage usernames and superusers.
XML Services shall monitor changes to data models recorded in the Registry.
XML Services shall validate tags against a style guide wizard.
XML Services shall generate a document that includes a schema.
XML Services shall develop a scheme for dynamic cross-referencing of XML objects.
XML Services shall check XML document conformance for well-formedness and validation.
XML Services shall disassemble documents to materialize query results into different tree structures.
XML Services shall transmit sufficient metadata to construct semantically-valid XSL views.
XML Services shall provide standard APIs for Registry Services as specified in Appendix B.
The Registry software shall be portable.
The Registry software shall execute on COE-compliant platforms.
The XS software shall be delivered in accordance with DII COE guidelines.
